summary: Chargate is a Python CLI that wraps MegaLinter and adds net-new (PR-diff) finding gating: on a PR only findings the diff introduced relative to the merge-base can block; the full unfiltered SARIF is always emitted and shipped (DefectDojo / Security tab / artifact). One `chargate` CLI backs three GitHub surfaces: a composite action (action.yml), a reusable workflow (gate.yml), and a pre-commit hook. The `sarif/` subpackage is a pure, deterministic, I/O-free core; `git.py` is the only subprocess boundary.

cli.purpose: argparse dispatch for the 4 subcommands (filter-sarif | ci | local | version); wires git -> filter -> gate -> report -> DefectDojo. The only module that touches os.environ + file writes for the CLI flow.

sarif.filter.purpose: CROWN JEWEL. Classify each SARIF result as net-new vs pre-existing against a DiffIndex under a FilterPolicy; return a pruned deep copy (net-new only) + per-result verdicts + Counts. Never mutates the input. Pure, no I/O. ResultVerdict carries the finding `message` + an `inline_safe` flag (added-line/new-file only) for picking valid inline PR-comment targets.

sarif.diff.purpose: Parse `git diff --unified=0 -M -C` text into a DiffIndex: per-file status (added/modified/deleted/renamed/copied) + added line ranges on the new side, read from hunk headers only. Pure.

sarif.model.purpose: Defensive, typed accessors over SARIF 2.1.0 result objects (primary uri, startLine, resolved level, security-severity, severity band). Tolerates missing/None fields. Pure.

sarif.counts.purpose: Tally totals + net-new + per-level + per-severity-band breakdowns. Takes net-new (run_index, result_index) keys (not verdicts) to avoid an import cycle with filter. Pure.

git.purpose: The ONLY git/subprocess boundary. Resolve merge-base(base, head), produce unified-diff text, detect shallow clones, and fail loudly with a fetch-depth hint. Keeps sarif/ pure.

gate.purpose: Turn a FilterResult into a pass/fail GateDecision against a fail_on threshold (any|critical|high|medium|low|none). Owns the exit-code constants and effective-severity-band logic.

megalinter.purpose: Build the MegaLinter env (DISABLE_ERRORS=true so it never gates; SARIF/JSON reporters on; normalized URIs) + docker run command, run it (runner injectable), and locate the merged SARIF tolerating filename ambiguity. Command assembly is pure/testable.

defectdojo.purpose: Optional, first-class DefectDojo import/reimport client (urllib + multipart). Always ships the FULL SARIF. Failure-isolated: import_sarif NEVER raises, returns ok=False so a DD failure can't fail the gate.

github_comment.purpose: Optional GHAS-style PR review-comment client (urllib, JSON). Posts net-new findings as ONE updatable summary comment (hidden-marker upsert) + inline review comments (delete-then-recreate, no stacking). Failure-isolated: post_pr_feedback NEVER raises, returns ok=False so a GitHub API error can't fail the gate. Pure transport; comment bodies are rendered by report.py.

modes.purpose: Resolve run mode from an explicit flag or GITHUB_EVENT_NAME: PR (net-new gate) vs BASELINE (full scan, no gate). Mode.gates says whether to apply the gate.

report.purpose: Output helpers: render the Markdown job summary + the PR summary/inline comment bodies (all pure, testable) + append to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY and write key=value GITHUB_OUTPUT (no-ops off Actions).

local.purpose: Fast pre-commit staged-file runner (gitleaks + ruff, each skipped if the tool is absent). Data-driven check registry; runner + which probes injectable. Deliberately narrower than CI.

callgraph_highlights.note: The full CI flow. Baseline mode skips the diff + gate (filters against an empty DiffIndex, fail_on=none). PR comments (net-new only) post on gate mode and never fail the gate. A MegaLinter tool error only fails the job under --strict.
The pure-ish filter subcommand: SARIF + base/head -> filtered SARIF + counts + gate exit code. Unit-tested in isolation from Actions.
classify_results -> _classify_one applies FilterPolicy per result; filter_sarif deep-copies and prunes runs[].results[] to the net-new keep-set.
The handoff from the IO boundary to the pure core: diff text in, DiffIndex out.
effective_band unifies security-severity band and SARIF level (error->high, warning->medium, note->low) for threshold comparison.

note_for_agents: Load this file on demand to locate code; it is NOT auto-imported into CLAUDE.md. Design rule: sarif/ stays pure (no subprocess/os/Actions imports) so it is unit-tested with synthetic diff text + SARIF dicts. Exit codes: 0 pass, 1 blocking net-new, 2 setup/tool error (a broken scanner is a tool error, never a finding). Tests mirror modules 1:1 under tests/ (e.g. test_sarif_filter.py, test_gate.py).
